description: This bar chart presents the Vickers hardness (HV) of different phases in as-cast Fe–30Mn–10Al–1.5C–xBe low-density steels, where x equals 0, 0.5, 1.0, 1.5 wt% and labeled as 0Be, 0.5Be, 1.0Be, 1.5Be respectively. Red bars represent austenite while blue bars represent ferrite. The measured values are: austenite hardness is 453.31 HV for 0Be, 462.88 HV for 0.5Be, 475.80 HV for 1.0Be, and 479.74 HV for 1.5Be; ferrite only forms in 1.0Be and 1.5Be dual-phase steels, with hardness of 437.40 HV and 452.54 HV respectively. Austenite hardness rises continuously with increasing Be content, and ferrite hardness also increases with Be content in dual-phase samples, while ferrite hardness is consistently lower than austenite hardness for the same Be addition. This figure confirms that Be addition effectively improves the matrix hardness of as-cast low-density steels, partially contributed by Be-containing precipitates, and ferrite formed at higher Be content shows lower hardness than austenite.
